visible_entities.desc: white rectangular page containing scientific graphs, charts, and text
graph with a rising curve labeled 'Fig. 9(a)'
graph with multiple curves and a diagram of a glider labeled 'Fig. 9(b)'
graph with a jagged line labeled 'A. NO-SPIN TOSS' and 'B. SPINNING TOSS' labeled 'Fig. 9(c)'
graph with multiple curves labeled 'Fig. 9(d)'
data table labeled 'Table 2'

atomic_facts.fact: The image displays a page of scientific data with four distinct graphs and one table.
The top-left graph shows 'R, m' on the y-axis and 'INITIAL SPINNING SPEED' on the x-axis.
The top-right graph shows 'R, m' versus 'INITIAL CROSS-PLANE ANGLE' and includes a diagram of a glider with vectors.
The bottom-left graph compares 'A. NO-SPIN TOSS' and 'B. SPINNING TOSS' trajectories.
Table 2 lists 'Throw range as affected by moment of inertia and spinning speed'.

ocr: Fig. 9(a) Effect of the initial spinning speed ω0 on range R (β1 = β2 = 0°)
Fig. 9(b) Effect of β1 and β2 on throw range R (initial speed v0, flight speed v1, and t1 taken as in Fig. 7; α0 and initial height)

Fig. 9(c) Trajectory and spinor description of the throw in Fig. 9(b) (initial speed v0 = 15.24 m/sec, flight speed v1 = 12.19 m/sec, t1 = 0.85 sec, initial height h0 = 1.83 m, α0 = 0°, β1 = 0°, β2 = 0°, ω0 = 10 rev/sec, and ω1 = 12 rev/sec)
Fig. 9(d) Effect of the initial speed v0 (relative to total wind velocity for no initial spin and no height loss) on throw range R (flight time T = 0.85 sec, h0 = 1.83 m, T = 0.85 sec)
Table 2 Throw range as affected by moment of inertia and spinning speed
spatial: The page is centered in the frame. Figure 9(a) is located in the top-left corner, while Figure 9(b) is in the top-right corner. Figure 9(c) is positioned below Figure 9(a) on the left side. Figure 9(d) is positioned below Figure 9(b) on the right side. Table 2 is located at the bottom center of the page, between the bottom two graphs.
state_changes: scientific graphs and data tables appear

visible_entities.desc: white page with black text and scientific graphs
graph plotting initial spinning speed vs Rm
graph plotting Rm vs initial discus in-plane angle
graph plotting Rm vs spin discus
graph plotting Rm vs wind velocity
table listing throwing range data

atomic_facts.fact: The document displays five distinct graphs related to discus throwing physics.
Figure 5a shows the effect of initial spinning speed on range.
Figure 5b shows the effect of in-plane angle on range.
Figure 5c compares no-spin and spin discus trajectories.
Figure 6 shows the effect of wind velocity on range.
Table 1 lists throwing ranges for different spin rates and angles.

ocr: Fig. 5a1 Effect of the initial spinning speed ω₀ on range R (K₁ = K₂ = K₃ = 0)
Fig. 5b1 Effect of K₁ and K₂ on the throw range. R shown versus angle K₁ fixed and K₂ varying, and vice versa. K₁ = K₃ and initial figure.
Fig. 5c1 Trajectory and velocity degradation of the discus in flight. Data...
Table 1 Throw range as affected by moment of inertia and spinning speed
Fig. 6 Effect of the wind velocity V_w (positive for head wind, positive for tail wind) on the range of a spinning discus (J/J₀, angles β₀, γ₀ = 35.0 degrees, V₀ = 22.5 m/sec, T₀ = 0.005 sec)
spatial: The document is centered on a dark blue background. Figure 5a is located at the top left of the page, while Figure 5b is at the top right. Figure 5c is positioned below Figure 5a, and Figure 6 is below Figure 5b. Table 1 is located at the bottom left corner of the page.

visible_entities.desc: white rectangular page containing scientific graphs, tables, and text with black ink
dark blue background with a subtle radial gradient

atomic_facts.fact: The page displays multiple line graphs plotting variables such as 'R, m' against 'INITIAL SPINNING SPEED' and 'INITIAL DISCUS-PLANE ANGLE'.
Table 2 is visible at the bottom left, listing numerical data for 'Spinning speed' and 'Flight time'.
The text 'Fig. 10(c)' is visible below the top-left graph.
The text 'Table 2 Throw range as affected by moment of inertia and spinning speed' is visible above the table.

ocr: Fig. 10(c) Effect of the initial spinning speed ω0 on range R (θ1 = θ2 = 0)
Fig. 10(d) Effect of θ1 and θ2 on the throw range. R: throw range; θ1: fixed and θ2 varies; and θ1 = θ2: both vary together.

Fig. 10(e) Variation of release conditions as the discus in flight loses angular momentum (θ1 = 0 deg, θ2 = 0 deg, ω0 = 60 rad/sec, V0 = 24.4 m/sec, h0 = 1.83 m, m = 2 kg, Ix = 0.004 kg-m2, Iy = 0.008 kg-m2, Iz = 0.008 kg-m2, S = 0.038 m2, initial AR = 1.6).
Table 2 Throw range as affected by moment of inertia and spinning speed

Fig. 11 Effect of the wind velocity Vw on the throw range. R: throw range; position for maximum range is given by θ1 = 0 deg and θ2 = 0 deg; angle β = 24.8 degrees; V0 = 24.4 m/sec; h0 = 1.83 m; ω0 = 60 rad/sec; Ix = 0.004 kg-m2; Iy = 0.008 kg-m2; Iz = 0.008 kg-m2; S = 0.038 m2; initial AR = 1.6.
spatial: The white page is centered on the dark blue background. Below these, 'Fig. 10(e)' is on the left and 'Fig. 11' is on the right. Table 2 is located at the bottom-left of the page, below 'Fig. 10(e)'.
